Uses of Interface
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.ArrayVerifier

Packages that use ArrayVerifier 
Package Description
com.github.cowwoc.requirements
The Requirements, DefaultRequirements endpoints.
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Classes used to verify preconditions, postconditions and invariants of core Java types.
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.extension
Interfaces that may be extended but not implemented (because the methods contain a generic return type).
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal
Design guide

  • Uses of ArrayVerifier in com.github.cowwoc.requirements

    Methods in com.github.cowwoc.requirements that return ArrayVerifier 
    Modifier and Type Method Description
    static ArrayVerifier<boolean[],​Boolean> DefaultRequirements.assertThat​(boolean[] actual, String name)
    Same as DefaultRequirements.requireThat(boolean[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    static ArrayVerifier<byte[],​Byte> DefaultRequirements.assertThat​(byte[] actual, String name)
    Same as DefaultRequirements.requireThat(byte[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    static ArrayVerifier<char[],​Character> DefaultRequirements.assertThat​(char[] actual, String name)
    Same as DefaultRequirements.requireThat(char[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    static ArrayVerifier<double[],​Double> DefaultRequirements.assertThat​(double[] actual, String name)
    Same as DefaultRequirements.requireThat(double[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    static ArrayVerifier<float[],​Float> DefaultRequirements.assertThat​(float[] actual, String name)
    Same as DefaultRequirements.requireThat(float[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    static ArrayVerifier<int[],​Integer> DefaultRequirements.assertThat​(int[] actual, String name)
    Same as DefaultRequirements.requireThat(int[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    static ArrayVerifier<long[],​Long> DefaultRequirements.assertThat​(long[] actual, String name)
    Same as DefaultRequirements.requireThat(long[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    static ArrayVerifier<short[],​Short> DefaultRequirements.assertThat​(short[] actual, String name)
    Same as DefaultRequirements.requireThat(short[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    static <E> ArrayVerifier<E[],​E> DefaultRequirements.assertThat​(E[] actual, String name)
    Same as DefaultRequirements.requireThat(Object[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    ArrayVerifier<boolean[],​Boolean> Requirements.assertThat​(boolean[] actual, String name)
    Same as Requirements.requireThat(boolean[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    ArrayVerifier<byte[],​Byte> Requirements.assertThat​(byte[] actual, String name)
    Same as Requirements.requireThat(byte[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    ArrayVerifier<char[],​Character> Requirements.assertThat​(char[] actual, String name)
    Same as Requirements.requireThat(char[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    ArrayVerifier<double[],​Double> Requirements.assertThat​(double[] actual, String name)
    Same as Requirements.requireThat(double[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    ArrayVerifier<float[],​Float> Requirements.assertThat​(float[] actual, String name)
    Same as Requirements.requireThat(float[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    ArrayVerifier<int[],​Integer> Requirements.assertThat​(int[] actual, String name)
    Same as Requirements.requireThat(int[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    ArrayVerifier<long[],​Long> Requirements.assertThat​(long[] actual, String name)
    Same as Requirements.requireThat(long[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    ArrayVerifier<short[],​Short> Requirements.assertThat​(short[] actual, String name)
    Same as Requirements.requireThat(short[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    <E> ArrayVerifier<E[],​E> Requirements.assertThat​(E[] actual, String name)
    Same as Requirements.requireThat(Object[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    static ArrayVerifier<boolean[],​Boolean> DefaultRequirements.requireThat​(boolean[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive boolean array.
    static ArrayVerifier<byte[],​Byte> DefaultRequirements.requireThat​(byte[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive byte array.
    static ArrayVerifier<char[],​Character> DefaultRequirements.requireThat​(char[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive char array.
    static ArrayVerifier<double[],​Double> DefaultRequirements.requireThat​(double[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive double array.
    static ArrayVerifier<float[],​Float> DefaultRequirements.requireThat​(float[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive float array.
    static ArrayVerifier<int[],​Integer> DefaultRequirements.requireThat​(int[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive int array.
    static ArrayVerifier<long[],​Long> DefaultRequirements.requireThat​(long[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive long array.
    static ArrayVerifier<short[],​Short> DefaultRequirements.requireThat​(short[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive short array.
    static <E> ArrayVerifier<E[],​E> DefaultRequirements.requireThat​(E[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of an Object array.

    Verifies the requirements of a primitive boolean array.

    Verifies the requirements of a primitive byte array.

    Verifies the requirements of a primitive short array.

    Verifies the requirements of an Object array.

  • Uses of ArrayVerifier in com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java

    Methods in com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java that return ArrayVerifier 
    Modifier and Type Method Description
    ArrayVerifier<E[],​E> CollectionVerifier.asArray​(Class<E> type)
    Returns a verifier for the actual value as an array.
    ArrayVerifier<boolean[],​Boolean> JavaRequirements.assertThat​(boolean[] actual, String name)
    Same as JavaRequirements.requireThat(boolean[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.

    Same as JavaRequirements.requireThat(byte[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.

    Same as JavaRequirements.requireThat(char[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.

    Same as JavaRequirements.requireThat(Object[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.

    Method parameters in com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java with type arguments of type ArrayVerifier 
    Modifier and Type Method Description
    CollectionVerifier<C,​E> CollectionVerifier.asArray​(Class<E> type, Consumer<ArrayVerifier<E[],​E>> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.

    Modifier and Type Method Description
    ArrayVerifier<E[],​E> ExtensibleCollectionVerifier.asArray​(Class<E> type)
    Returns a verifier for the actual value as an array.

    Modifier and Type Method Description
    S ExtensibleCollectionVerifier.asArray​(Class<E> type, Consumer<ArrayVerifier<E[],​E>> consumer)
    Validates nested requirements.

    Classes in com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal that implement ArrayVerifier 
    Modifier and Type Class Description
    class  ArrayVerifierImpl<A,​E>
    Default implementation of ArrayVerifier.
    class  ArrayVerifierNoOp<A,​E>
    An ArrayVerifier that does nothing.
